title: Fuser Error
fullDescription: Error 900.00 indicates the fuser unit is not operating correctly. The fuser applies heat and pressure to bond toner to paper.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug for 30 seconds, then restart.

fixSteps.title: Clear Jams
fixSteps.instruction: Open all covers and remove any jammed paper.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Supplies
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reinstall toner and imaging supplies.

fixSteps.title: Check Firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Check for firmware updates on Lexmark's support website.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power cycling and basic troubleshooting.

faq.a: The fuser assembly has malfunctioned.
Many Lexmark errors can be resolved with basic troubleshooting.

faq.q: Where do I find Lexmark support?
faq.a: Visit support.lexmark.com for your model's specific documentation.

title: Paper Jam
shortDescription: Paper is jammed in the printer.
fullDescription: Error 200 indicates a paper jam has been detected. The paper must be removed before printing can resume.

fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug for 30 seconds, then restart.

fixSteps.title: Clear Jams

title: Paper Short - Reload Paper
shortDescription: The paper did not reach the exit sensor as expected.
fullDescription: Error 34 indicates paper was too short or did not complete its path through the printer, usually due to a misfeed.

causes.cause: Paper too small for selected size
